steps.title: Identify the subject's number
steps.content: The main subject is “Food writer J. Kenji López-Alt”—one person. The relative clause “who explores the science behind cooking” modifies the subject but does not change its number.

steps.title: Apply subject-verb agreement
steps.content: Explains is the third-person singular present-tense form that satisfies subject-verb agreement. Choices B, C, and D violate the rule: are and have are plural auxiliaries, and explain is the plural/base form. This matches choice A — A is correct.
